minFeeActiveTvlRatio: Minimum fee-to-active-TVL ratio a pool must show to be considered. Higher = pickier (fewer, higher-fee pools pass). This is window-dependent: the same number means something very different on a 5m window vs a 24h window — check your `timeframe` setting before tuning this.
minTvl: Minimum pool TVL (USD) to consider. Too low lets in illiquid pools with high slippage; too high excludes fresh/small opportunities.
maxTvl: Maximum pool TVL (USD). Very large pools tend to have thin fee/TVL ratios — this caps how 'big and lazy' a pool can be.
minVolume: Minimum trading volume (USD) over the screening timeframe. Low volume means fees will trickle in slowly even if the ratio looks good.
minOrganic: Minimum organic-volume score (0-100) — filters out pools whose volume looks wash-traded/bot-driven rather than real activity.
minHolders: Minimum token holder count. Too few holders is a common early rug-pull signal.
minMcap: Minimum token market cap (USD). Filters out extremely small/illiquid tokens.
maxMcap: Maximum token market cap (USD). Very large-cap tokens tend to have thinner fee yield relative to TVL.
minBinStep: Minimum Meteora bin step (price granularity) allowed. Lower bin step = tighter price bins = more sensitive to price moves.
maxBinStep: Maximum bin step allowed. Higher bin step = wider price bins = less rebalancing needed but less capital efficiency.
timeframe: The screening window (5m/30m/1h/2h/4h/12h/24h). Changing this auto-rescales minFeeActiveTvlRatio/minVolume to sane defaults for that window unless you set those explicitly in the same change.
minTokenFeesSol: Minimum total priority+jito fees (in SOL) paid by the token's traders. Below this threshold often indicates bundled/scripted volume, not real trading.
maxBotHoldersPct: Maximum % of holders flagged as bot addresses (Jupiter audit). Higher = more tolerant of bot activity.
maxTop10Pct: Maximum % of supply held by the top 10 wallets. High concentration = one whale can crash the price.
blockedLaunchpads: List of launchpad names to hard-exclude before the AI even sees candidates from them (e.g. pump.fun).
deployAmountSol: Floor (minimum) SOL amount used for a new position, before compounding scales it up with wallet growth.
maxDeployAmount: Ceiling (maximum) SOL amount for a single position, regardless of wallet size.
maxPositions: Maximum number of concurrent open positions allowed. Reaching this cap is what feeds the free 'shadow-skip' simulation — good candidates skipped purely due to capacity.
gasReserve: SOL kept aside and never deployed, to guarantee enough for transaction fees.
positionSizePct: % of deployable wallet balance (after gas reserve) used per new position — this is what makes position size compound as the wallet grows.
minSolToOpen: Minimum wallet SOL balance required before the bot will even attempt to open a new position.
outOfRangeWaitMinutes: How long a position can sit out-of-range before the bot considers closing it.
stopLossPct: PnL % at which a position is force-closed to cut losses (negative number, e.g. -50 = close at -50%).
takeProfitPct: PnL % at which a position is closed to lock in profit.
trailingTakeProfit: If enabled, once PnL crosses trailingTriggerPct, the bot tracks the peak and closes if PnL drops trailingDropPct from that peak — locks in gains without capping upside too early.
trailingTriggerPct: PnL % at which trailing take-profit tracking activates.
trailingDropPct: % drop from the tracked peak PnL that triggers a trailing-stop close.
managementIntervalMin: How often (minutes) the management cycle checks open positions for close/rebalance conditions.
screeningIntervalMin: How often (minutes) the screener looks for new pools to deploy into.
solMode: If enabled, PnL/values are reported in SOL terms instead of USD.
simAbTestEnabled: Turns on the paper-trading A/B test: the screener also evaluates candidates against `simAltConfig`'s thresholds in parallel and tracks a simulated position when the result differs from live. Costs at most one extra LLM call per screening cycle when it diverges — see the token-usage panel for the actual cost.
simAltConfig: The alternate threshold set used only by the A/B test (never affects real deploys). Any of the screening keys above can be overridden here to try a different strategy against the same live market data.
